hard.next_step: 6.29.26 KK - had commercial call. Pricing well received. They understand the timeline is tight and they need to make a decision in the coming week or so. RISK- coupa is going onsite wednesday.
hard.ais_why: AI importance logged 'Neutral'; buyer's action items across nearly three hours of recorded discussion were strictly pricing, P2P volumes and T&E, not AI or Merlin. Merlin/Agentic product tags are Zycus-side, not buyer-voiced.
hard.competitor: Coupa (incumbent); Do Nothing / Renew Coupa; Zip (faded)

north_star_verdict.headline: Live Coupa-replacement in active commercial negotiation: David Elford is engaged and pricing 'well received' on 29 Jun, but 30 Jul slips unless the overdue P2P change-management references land before Coupa's on-site and the CFO/back-half gates get scheduled.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 12 Mar 2026, ~3.9 months in cycle; Shortlisted since 27 May 2026 (~6 weeks in stage); close 30 Jul is 24 days out. Engagement is real: commercial call 29 Jun (pricing well received) and two-way email 25 Jun. But the decisive proof, the Coupa-replacement P2P references David requested 27 May, is ~40 days overdue; no CFO/CPO is engaged; and back-half gates (EB sign-off, legal, InfoSec, SOW/MSA, legacy-ERP integration) are unscheduled and cannot realistically complete by 30 Jul. David's ~6-7 month implementation vs the ~Feb 2027 Coupa renewal keeps a near-term signature real, not artificial, so this is a timing problem on a healthy deal, not a stall.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Best Case (not Commit): pricing landed and the buyer is moving, but references are overdue, no economic buyer is engaged, scope was cut ~20% on 12 Jun, and no contracting path is scheduled inside 24 days.
evidence: SF Next_Step__c 29 Jun 2026 (Karson Keogh): commercial call held, pricing well received, buyer must decide 'in the coming week or so'; RISK flagged that Coupa is going on-site Wednesday

David Elford, proposal call 6 Apr 2026: 'I have no doubt Coupa can serve our business or you can serve our business. The issue is change management' — the decision hinges on adoption proof, not product
David Elford, demo 27 May 2026: 'at this point it's pretty much down to you and the incumbent... by the end of June we need to make a decision' — Zycus is one of the final two on a fixed clock
Field history: Amount reduced 556,500 -> 445,200 on 12 Jun 2026 and ownership reassigned Mario Castro -> Karson Keogh on 26 Jun 2026 — scope narrowing plus a continuity risk 24 days from close

metrics.narrative: The buyer's pain is concrete and the deal is well-scoped in numbers, but no Zycus-specific ROI case is built yet. On the 6 Apr 2026 proposal call David Elford confirmed the licensing basis himself: ~$250M annual P2P spend against $1.04B total spend, ~1,500 procurement/intake users, ~1,000 contracts and ~225,000 invoices, and on 27 May he sized the change: 'Upstream touches round numbers 20 people. Downstream touches 2,000 people.' The operational pain is degraded analytics ('they changed their data visualization engine two, maybe 3 years ago... it was a step backwards', David, 27 May) and fragmented intake David said they lack today. The gap is that no dollarized savings/efficiency model has been built against those figures, so quantifying the analytics and adoption gains into a number David can carry to his CFO is the move.
sources: Avoma proposal call 6 Apr 2026 (David Elford licensing assumptions)
Avoma demo 27 May 2026 (David Elford: 2,000 downstream users, degraded analytics)

economic_buyer.narrative: David Elford (Director, Non-Merchandise Procurement) is the active budget authority: he negotiates term length and pricing directly ('get us a 5 year price', 6 Apr), sets scope ('we'd go all the way to the right to the full declarative platform', 6 Apr) and directs the process ('the process should move under his direction', per Next_Step__c 22 Jun). Above him, a CFO mandated the evaluation, per David on the 30 Mar demo ('CFO challenged procurement to evaluate market alternatives'), but that CFO has never joined a call, reviewed the proposal or validated budget, and David has gated executive access. So the person who controls the commercial today is mapped and engaged, while the ultimate sign-off authority on the $445K, five-year commitment is unmapped 24 days from close. The move is a peer exec connect (Alexa Bradley to the CFO / David's boss at CPO level) to validate budget and the Feb 2027 timeline before Coupa's on-site.
sources: Avoma proposal call 6 Apr 2026 (David Elford drives pricing and scope)
Avoma demo 30 Mar 2026 (David: CFO mandated the market evaluation)
SF Next_Step__c 22 Jun 2026 (David gatekeeping executive access, escalation to CFO/CPO flagged)

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are explicit from the buyer. Primary is downstream P2P change management at 2,000-user scale: David on 27 May said 'where I'm most nervous is your downstream P2P... getting that many people comfortable with the change management and the usability of the tool' and asked specifically for references who were 'previous Coupa customers' to hear 'the transition, how their organization adapted and what change management was involved.' Second is T&E parity without disruption: keeping Egencia and a mobile-first experience, which the 27 May Emburse demo satisfied ('you've convinced me... it's very similar to what we have today'). Third is unified spend visibility and single intake to fix Coupa's degraded analytics, and fourth is integrating the legacy AS400 payables ledgers (Infinium, KBM, IDS) plus Timberline and Workday. Product fit on iSource/iContract/iSupplier is still to be validated (David: 'we didn't really look in detail at your iSource, iContract and iSupplier modules').
sources: Avoma demo 27 May 2026 (David Elford: change-management criterion, reference ask, ERP integration concern)
Avoma proposal call 6 Apr 2026 (David: modules not yet reviewed)

decision_process.narrative: The process reads as a two-finalist down-select on a self-imposed clock. David stated on 27 May 'this is part of that evaluation... it's pretty much down to you and the incumbent... by the end of June we need to make a decision,' and the 29 Jun commercial call ended with the buyer needing to decide 'in the coming week or so.' The internal chain: David socializes internally (he asked for a couple of weeks to 'socialize this' on 6 Apr), engages his AP team (nervous about change) and IT (built the current integrations), with a CFO/CPO sign-off above him that has not yet been activated. What is unconfirmed is the formal back-half sequence, no legal, InfoSec or SOW/MSA steps are scheduled, so even a 'yes' in the coming week leaves an unscoped path to signature. Reading the call sequence, seniority has NOT risen (Chris Rodgers joined the 27 May demo but no CFO/CPO has appeared), which is the main process risk.
sources: Avoma demo 27 May 2026 (David: down to final two, end-of-June decision)
SF Next_Step__c 29 Jun 2026 (decide in the coming week)
Avoma proposal call 6 Apr 2026 (David: socialize internally, AP alignment needed)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are not yet visible, which is the credibility gap on the 30 Jul date. A full five-year software proposal (~$445K annual) plus implementation (professional services quoted at 1.1-1.3x software, amortized over ~18 months, per Alexa Bradley 6 Apr) has been delivered and 'well received', but there is no evidence of MSA/Order Form drafting, legal redlines, an InfoSec/security review, or SOW scoping being scheduled. The natural forcing function is the ~Feb 2027 Coupa contract expiration (David extended Coupa one year 'to give us a chance to look at alternatives') against a 6-7 month implementation, which David acknowledged means 'if we were to try to switch, we got to get going soon.' What would create visibility is a co-authored decision-and-contracting plan naming the legal, InfoSec and SOW owners and dates.
sources: Avoma proposal call 6 Apr 2026 (Alexa Bradley: PS 1.1-1.3x, milestone billing)
Avoma demo 27 May 2026 (David: Feb 2027 Coupa expiration, 6-7 month runway)

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is confirmed and owner-attributed. David Elford and Whitney Parks own the pain: degraded spend analytics ('the data is there... but accessing it is not easy', David 27 May), fragmented intake ('a single intake for all the different types of buying... we don't have it today, but that would be a big improvement', David 6 Apr), slow Coupa support, and the friction of consolidating spend across the imburse/T&E and Zycus environments. Whitney owns the analytics/commodity-coding and reporting pain (she pushed on capturing commodity, GL and capital allocations on the 27 May call). The confirmed pain is real but moderate: David is explicit the incumbent 'can serve our business,' so the compelling event is less product pain than the Feb 2027 renewal decision plus the risk of staying on degraded tooling.
sources: Avoma demo 27 May 2026 (David: analytics access pain; Whitney: commodity/GL allocation needs)
Avoma proposal call 6 Apr 2026 (David: no single intake today)

champion.narrative: David Elford (Director, Non-Merchandise Procurement) is a developing champion: he drives the evaluation, declared full-suite intent ('for 100,000 more, I think it would be foolish of us not to consider going all the way', 6 Apr), controls scheduling and named Zycus one of the final two. Whitney Parks (Non Merchandise Procurement Manager) is an engaged supporting advocate handling requirements and volume data. What has developed is clear product preference, live commercial engagement and a stated decision timeline. What is still missing is proven upward advocacy: David has gatekept executive access ('the process should move under his direction') and has not introduced the CFO/CPO or brought his change-averse AP team into alignment. The conversion play is to arm David with the Coupa-replacement references and a change-management plan as the artifacts he carries to his CFO, turning a gatekeeper into a sponsor.
sources: Avoma proposal call 6 Apr 2026 (David: full-suite intent)
Avoma demo 27 May 2026 (David: down to final two, drives timeline)
SF Next_Step__c 22 Jun 2026 (David gatekeeping exec access)

competition.narrative: This is a Coupa-incumbent displacement plus a 'do nothing / renew Coupa' risk, and the incumbent is actively defending. Coupa has been in for ~6 years and remains the default fallback: David said on 6 Apr 'I have no doubt Coupa can serve our business... the issue is change management,' which makes inertia the real competitor. The 29 Jun Next Step flags a fresh threat, 'Coupa is going on-site Wednesday', so the incumbent will reinforce the relationship and reframe the decision around switching risk before Zycus has delivered its overdue references. Zip was mentioned once as a previously-evaluated intake tool but never onboarded (Corey Cooper, 12 Mar) and is dormant. Emburse is NOT a competitor, it is Zycus's strategic T&E partner in the proposal. Zycus wins by de-risking change management with Coupa-replacement P2P references and a contractual adoption plan delivered BEFORE the on-site, leaning on single-code-base, Teams-native guided buying and unified spend visibility versus Coupa's acquisition-stitched, degraded analytics and slow post-acquisition support.
sources: Avoma proposal call 6 Apr 2026 (David Elford: Coupa can serve us, change management is the issue)
SF Next_Step__c 29 Jun 2026 (Coupa going on-site)
Avoma intro call 12 Mar 2026 (Zip evaluated, never onboarded)

deal_movement.summary: Genuinely engaged and re-accelerating after a mid-June lull: a commercial call on 29 Jun landed pricing well and the buyer signalled a decision in the coming week, but the amount was cut ~20% on 12 Jun and ownership was reassigned on 26 Jun, and the decisive references remain undelivered.

items.change: Commercial/pricing call held with David Elford and Whitney Parks; pricing well received, buyer to decide 'in the coming week'
Ownership reassigned Mario Castro -> Karson Keogh; division moved Zycus -> New Markets
Two-way email re-engagement; David booked the proposal review for Mon 29 Jun
Amount reduced 556,500 -> 445,200 after proposal 'adjustments based on your feedback'
Full 5-year, full-suite proposal (Zycus + Emburse T&E) delivered
Stage advanced Formal Evaluation -> Shortlisted; buyer confirms 'down to you and the incumbent'
Emburse T&E demo delivered; David: 'you've convinced me... very similar to what we have today'

competitive_position.summary: Coupa is the ~6-year incumbent and the single strongest CURRENT threat: David admits it 'can serve our business' and the only real obstacle is change management, and it is actively defending with an on-site visit this week (29 Jun Next Step) before Zycus delivered its overdue adoption-proof references. The twin threat is 'renew Coupa / do nothing', the realistic status-quo since the pain is moderate. Zip was floated once as an intake option (12 Mar) but never onboarded and is dormant; 'Others' in Competitors__c is an unnamed data artifact. Emburse is our T&E partner, not a rival.

best_practice_check.summary: Three levers decide this deal in the next two weeks: land the change-management proof before Coupa's on-site, open the never-engaged economic buyer, and clarify the ~20% scope cut.
flags: Change management is the whole game: David's only real blocker is 2,000-user downstream P2P adoption vs Coupa, so land the Coupa-replacement references before Coupa's on-site or the incumbent reframes the decision around switching risk.

No executive thread 24 days from close: the CFO who mandated the eval and David's CPO-level boss have never engaged, so open a peer connect (Alexa Bradley to the CFO) to validate the $445K budget and the Feb 2027 cutover timeline.

Scope narrowed ~20% (556.5K -> 445.2K on 12 Jun): confirm whether the buyer is phasing the rollout or trimming budget, and re-anchor on the full-suite ROI David himself favoured ('foolish not to go all the way', 6 Apr).

It is under-positioned, but with an AI-Resistant buyer the near-term lever is change-management proof, not an AI pitch.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: We're one of two finalists in a Coupa displacement at Bass Pro with an engaged procurement champion (David Elford) and pricing 'well received' on the 29 Jun call, but the 30 Jul close hinges on landing the ~40-day-overdue P2P change-management references before Coupa's on-site and getting a never-engaged CFO to validate the reduced $445K budget.

win_position.text: Why this number: Shortlisted (final two) caps confidence around 70; the deal earns roughly mid-60s on a strong T&E win, David's full-suite intent and a developing champion, but is held below the cap by an unengaged economic buyer and a 20% scope cut, so it lands mid, not high.
David Elford is a developing champion driving it: he told us on 27 May 'it's pretty much down to you and the incumbent' and pricing was 'well received' on the 29 Jun commercial call.

Coupa is a 6-year incumbent David admits 'can serve our business' (6 Apr) and is going on-site this week to defend, so the overdue Coupa-replacement references are the only thing that de-risks the switch.
No CFO or CPO has engaged 24 days from close and David has gatekept executive access, so budget sign-off on the $445K, five-year commitment is unvalidated.

deal_momentum.text: Commercial call held 29 Jun with pricing well received and the buyer signalling a decision 'in the coming week', with the ~Feb 2027 Coupa renewal and a 6-7 month implementation forcing urgency.
The account went unresponsive for about a week in mid-June and ownership was reassigned Mario Castro -> Karson Keogh on 26 Jun, both risks to continuity this close to the date.

customer_commitment.text: David declared full-suite intent on 6 Apr ('foolish of us not to consider going all the way') and committed to a decision by end of June / the coming week.
The amount was cut ~20% (556.5K -> 445.2K) on 12 Jun after proposal adjustments, a sign of budget defensiveness or a move to phase the rollout rather than a big-bang full-suite buy.

deal_risk.text: The Coupa-replacement P2P references David requested on 27 May are ~40 days overdue while Coupa reinforces the relationship on-site this week.
The 2,000-user downstream P2P change management that is David's stated primary fear is unresolved and unproven.
Back-half gates, CFO sign-off, legal, InfoSec, SOW/MSA and the legacy AS400/Timberline/Workday integrations, are all unscheduled inside 24 days to the 30 Jul close.

business_case.evidence: Pain and scale quantified (~$250M P2P spend, 2,000 downstream users, degraded analytics) but no Zycus-specific ROI/savings model built yet.
